channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: NIS2, è scattata l’ora della compliance. Ma la corsa dei CIO non è finita
item.description: Il 28 febbraio è scattata l’ora X per la NIS2: entro questa scadenza le imprese che rientrano nel perimetro della Direttiva europea “Network and Information Security 2” (che ha aggiornato le norme dell’UE sulla cybersicurezza) si sono dovute qualificare e registrare sulla piattaforma messa a disposi
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Trump’s crypto reserve is a payoff for loyalists
item.description: President Donald Trump has found a new way to reward his supporters. On Sunday, Trump announced in a Truth Social post that he will issue an executive order creating a “Crypto Strategic Reserve” that will include Bitcoin, Ethereum, and three other cryptocurrencies. “I will make sure the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Scott Nover, Fast Company

item.title: EV-maker BYD seeks to raise $5.2 billion with share sale
item.description: Chinese electric vehicle maker BYD has launched a sale of its Hong Kong shares to raise up to $5.2 billion via an accelerated book-building, according to a deal term sheet seen by Reuters on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: AI Chatbots have telltale quirks. Researchers can spot them with 97% accuracy
item.description: There’s a cat-and-mouse game between those using generative AI chatbots to produce text undetected and those trying to catch them. Many believe they know the telltale signs—though as a journalist fond of the word “delve” and prone to em-dashes, I’m not so sure. Researchers at four U.S.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: US Cybercom, CISA retreat in fight against Russian cyber threats: reports
item.description: Two reports published on Friday signaled the Trump administration may no longer consider Russia the US government’s top cyber foe, a development that would radically alter long-standing American global alliances to defend against nation-state threat actors in the digital realm.
item.dc:creator: csoonline

item.title: US businesses are falling behind when it comes to digital transformation
item.description: These sectors are falling behind when it comes to digital transformation, and SMBs are likely at a higher risk.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Americans split over Musk, federal workforce cuts: Survey
item.description: Americans in a new survey are split over their views of Elon Musk and his Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE), as well as federal workforce cuts during the opening weeks of the second Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: New malware exploits fake updates to steal data
item.description: New malware "FrigidStealer" targets Mac computers. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson offers four ways to stay safe from infostealer malware.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: I’d love a laptop powered by the sun... and Lenovo’s Yoga Solar PC at MWC 2025 looks like a sizeable step towards that dream
item.description: A highly efficient solar panel in a laptop lid? Lenovo’s Yoga Solar PC is turning heads at MWC 2025 and it’s a genuinely exciting new laptop concept.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: UK data protection watchdog investigating how TikTok uses children's personal data
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The U.K.'s data protection watchdog said Monday that it's investigating how TikTok uses the personal information of teenagers to deliver content recommendations to them when they use the social media platform.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIA HUI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Here's when you can stream Anora, this year's huge Oscar-winning smash hit
item.description: Anora championed the 2025 Oscars, winning Best Actress, Best Director, and Best Picture. Here's where and when you can stream it this month.
item.dc:creator: rowan.davies@futurenet.com (Rowan Davies), TechRadar

item.title: Breaking up Intel is the ‘dumbest idea’ around: Former CEO Craig Barett
item.description: In a blistering critique, former Intel CEO and Chairman Craig Barrett has strongly opposed breaking the company into separate design and foundry units, arguing that Intel’s recent technological resurgence positions it to challenge TSMC’s dominance in the semiconductor industry without corporate dism
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Who at DOGE has access to U.S. intelligence secrets? Democrats are demanding answers
item.description: Democratic lawmakers demanded answers from billionaire Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency on Thursday as concerns about who has access to America’s most sensitive information continue to dog Trump administration efforts to reshape the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Google co-founder: Be in the office every weekday, work 60 hours a week
item.description: Google co-founder Sergey Brin sent an internal message Wednesday to the group working on the company’s AI model Gemini. In the message, Brin wrote that Google can become a leader in AI development — provided that employees work more.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Google’s AI summaries are changing search. Now it’s facing a lawsuit
item.description: For more than two decades, users have turned to search engines like Google, typed in a query, and received a familiar list of 10 blue links—the gateway to the wider web. Ranking high on that list, through search engine optimization (SEO), has become a $200 billion business.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: The Dark Side of Onboarding: Why Secure Identity Verification Matters
item.description: The importance of secure identity verification in onboarding employees and contractors, preventing security threats and financial losses.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Google’s AlloyDB is looking more and more like PostgreSQL
item.description: Google is adding new features to its fully managed database-as-a-service (DBaaS) AlloyDB in order to provide enterprises with an alternative to PostgreSQL, which has become the most popular choice when transitioning from legacy database management systems.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: AI & Data Exchange 2025: GAO’s Lindsey Saul on AI helping speed data analytics, improve oversight
item.description: AI presents opportunities for greater efficiency, but GAO chief data officer says its effectiveness hinges on data quality. The post AI & Data Exchange 2025: GAO’s Lindsey Saul on AI helping speed data analytics, improve oversight first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jory Heckman, Federal News Network

item.title: Network jobs watch: Hiring, skills and certification trends
item.description: Network and infrastructure roles continue to shift as enterprises adopt technologies such as AI-driven network operations, multicloud networking, zero trust network access (ZTNA), and SD-WAN.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Android phones to drive mobile sales in 2025
item.description: Global sales of smartphones will increase by 2.3% in 2025 compared to last year, according to a new report from IDC. Android-based phones are expected to account for most of the increase, including in China, where sales are expected to rise by 5.6% year over year.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: One Tech Tip: Getting a lot of unwanted phone calls? Here are ways to stop them
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Unwanted phone calls are out of control. Whether it's a robocall trying to sell you something or spam calls from scammers trying to rip you off, it's enough to make you want to stop answering your phone.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Nvidia sales surge in the fourth quarter on demand for AI chips
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Nvidia on Wednesday reported a surge in fourth-quarter profit and sales as demand for its specialized Blackwell chips, which power artificial intelligence systems, continued to grow, sending the company's stock higher after hours. For the three months that ended Jan.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hackers slam Michigan tribe for not negotiating after ransomware attack
item.description: A group called RansomHub has claimed responsibility for the cyberattack and submitted a letter to provide its “side of the events.
item.dc:creator: Matt Jaworowski, NewsNation

item.title: Plug-and-play web development with Astro
item.description: Astro is a full-stack JavaScript meta-framework that orchestrates reactive view technologies like React and Svelte. While there are numerous meta-frameworks (examples include Next.
item.dc:creator: Info World
